summary: This notebook demonstrates an efficient inference pipeline for Kaggle's MAP competition using the Gemma2-9B-it large language model. It shows how to load a pre-trained base model, apply a LoRA adapter for weight-efficient updates, and generate predictions on test data. The author highlights key training tips, such as using LoRA/QLORA for VRAM efficiency and switching to fp16 for T4 GPU compatibility. Readers learn how to format tabular/text data into LLM prompts, tokenize inputs, and compute a custom MAP@3 metric for evaluation. The notebook serves as a quick submission recipe rather than a full training guide.
competition_flow: Loads train/test CSVs, engineers a frequency-based correctness feature, formats prompts for Gemma2-9B-it, tokenizes inputs, loads a LoRA-wrapped model, computes top-3 class probabilities, and exports a space-separated submission CSV.
data_reading: pd.read_csv for train.csv and test.csv; fills NaN in Misconception with 'NA'.
data_processing: Tokenization with transformers.AutoTokenizer (max_length=256, padding=max_length, truncation=True); converts to HuggingFace Dataset with PyTorch format; uses fp16 for inference on T4 GPUs.
features_engineering: Creates an is_correct feature by identifying the most frequently selected MC_Answer per QuestionId in the training set and merging it back; formats input prompts combining QuestionText, MC_Answer, is_correct (Yes/No), and StudentExplanation.
insights: Using a larger 9B parameter model yields better CV/LB scores than smaller 1B alternatives.
LoRA adapters enable efficient fine-tuning and inference of large LLMs on constrained GPU memory.
Switching to fp16 is necessary and accelerates inference on Kaggle's T4 GPUs since they lack bf16 support.
Carefully crafting the input prompt structure can significantly impact validation scores.
critical_findings: The reported CV score is actually derived from a single 20% holdout split, not a true cross-validation process.
Kaggle's T4 GPUs do not support bf16, requiring fp16 for inference to avoid errors and improve speed.
